title: Pasupateeswarar Temple, Karur
tagline: Pasupateeswarar Temple, Karur — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Pasupateeswarar temple, Karur is located in Karur and is one of the seven Sivastalams.
overview.description: Pasupateeswarar temple, Karur is located in Karur and is one of the seven Sivastalams. This city was called karuvoor during the time of Sambandar's visit to this temple. From the inscriptions so far deciphered, it is clear that the temple existed during the reign of the king Rajendra Chola (1012-54 CE) since he gifted land to this temple. For the Kongu Cholas and Kongu Pandyas, the temple was a favourite and, it the drew undivided attention of the Vijayanagar rulers too.

topPlaces.name: 2025 Karur crowd crush
topPlaces.description: On 27 September 2025, at least 41 people were killed and around 100 others were injured in a crowd crush during a political rally in Karur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The rally was hosted by Vijay, the founder and president of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK).

items.note: Govindhampalayam is a village in Karur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu situated on the banks of Cauvery.
Karur division is a revenue division in the Karur district of Tamil Nadu, India.
Karur taluk is a taluk of Karur district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
The Amaravati River is the longest tributary of the Kaveri River in the fertile districts of Udumalpet, Dharapuram and Karur Tamil Nadu state, South India.

Karur (Tamil: [kaɾuːɾ]) is a city and municipal corporation in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is the administrative headquarters of Karur district and is administered by the Karur Municipal Corporation.
Thanthoni is a block in Karur Municipal Corporation in Karur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. As of 2011, the town had a population of 53,854..
Inam Karur is a municipality in Karur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. As of 2011, the town had a population of 67,131.

On 27 September 2025, at least 41 people were killed and around 100 others were injured in a crowd crush during a political rally in Karur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The rally was hosted by Vijay, the founder and president of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pasupateeswarar Temple, Karur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pasupateeswarar Temple, Karur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pasupateeswarar Temple, Karur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (71 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (68 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pasupateeswarar Temple, Karur?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pasupateeswarar Temple, Karur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pasupateeswarar Temple, Karur?
faq.a: Stays in Pasupateeswarar Temple, Karur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pasupateeswarar Temple, Karur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Govindhampalayam, Karur division, Karur taluk, Amaravati River, Karur.
